name: Abegondo - Cecebre - Biosphere Reserve - figure of 8 loop
name_original: Abegondo - Cecebre - Reserva da Biosfera - figura de 8 bucle
region_intro: Road cycling around Encoro de Cecebre offers a network of no traffic routes through a protected natural space. The region is characterized by the reservoir formed by the Mero and Barcés rivers, surrounded by extensive riparian forests, marsh vegetation, and small oak forests. These natural features create a varied environment with mostly paved surfaces, ideal for quiet road cycling. The landscape includes wetland ecosystems and gentle slopes, providing diverse terrain for cyclists. Best no traffic road cycling routes around Encoro de Cecebre The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Abegondo - Cecebre - Biosphere Reserve - figure of 8 loop, a 53.9 miles (86.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the wider biosphere reserve, offering extensive views of the natural landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cecebre, a moderate 35.4 miles (57.0 km) path. This route provides scenic views around the reservoir, passing through varied terrain with moderate elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from A Brea, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through riparian forests and along the reservoir's edge, often completed in about 46 minutes. Road cycling around Encoro de Cecebre is defined by the reservoir, riparian forests, and wetland ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Encoro de Cecebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 3,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Encoro de Cecebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available around Encoro de Cecebre?
A: There are over 20 no-traffic road cycling routes available in the Encoro de Cecebre area, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy loops to more challenging long-distance rides.

Q: What makes Encoro de Cecebre ideal for no-traffic road cycling?
A: Encoro de Cecebre offers a unique combination of serene landscapes and quiet, mostly paved roads, making it perfect for no-traffic road cycling. As a protected natural space, it provides a tranquil environment with scenic views of the reservoir and its surrounding riparian forests, ensuring a peaceful ride away from heavy vehicle traffic.

Q: When is the best time of year to enjoy no-traffic road cycling in Encoro de Cecebre?
A: The Encoro de Cecebre area is generally pleasant for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery, with lush greenery or vibrant fall colors. Summers are also popular, but it's advisable to start earlier in the day to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly no-traffic road cycling routes in Encoro de Cecebre?
A: Yes, Encoro de Cecebre has several easy routes suitable for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from A Brea&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, offering a gentle ride around the reservoir with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Can I find circular no-traffic road cycling routes around Encoro de Cecebre?
A: Many of the no-traffic road cycling routes in Encoro de Cecebre are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15032277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cecebre&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive tour of the area's natural beauty.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: While cycling around Encoro de Cecebre, you'll encounter diverse natural features and points of interest. The reservoir itself, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5223936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, is a central highlight, surrounded by extensive riparian forests and marsh vegetation. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mero River Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a tranquil path along the river, and various bird observation cabins, as the area is a significant ornithological site.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting Encoro de Cecebre for a no-traffic road cycling trip?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points around the Encoro de Cecebre area. Specific parking locations can vary depending on your chosen starting point for a route. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for recommended parking spots near the trailhead.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the no-traffic road cycling routes in Encoro de Cecebre?
A: Encoro de Cecebre is located about 20 minutes from A Coruña. While direct public transport options specifically for cyclists might be limited, local bus services connect nearby towns. For the most convenient access to the cycling routes, driving and parking at a suitable trailhead is often recommended.

Q: Are there any challenging no-traffic road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Encoro de Cecebre offers more demanding no-traffic routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971550975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abegondo - Cecebre - Biosphere Reserve - figure of 8 loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering a significant distance and elevation, providing a rigorous workout amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: Are there cafes or places to eat near the no-traffic road cycling routes in Encoro de Cecebre?
A: While the immediate vicinity of the reservoir's quiet roads may have limited facilities, nearby towns and villages around Encoro de Cecebre offer cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel. It's a good idea to bring provisions for your ride, as some routes may lack fountains or rest areas directly along the path.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about no-traffic road cycling in Encoro de Cecebre?
A: The Encoro de Cecebre area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the quiet, traffic-free roads, the beautiful natural scenery, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable and peaceful cycling experience. The diverse terrain also caters to various skill levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides.
tips: Really quiet road with fast rolling asphalt and native forest, expect to ride surrounded by oaks, chestnut trees and poplars among others
The Abegondo-Cecebre Reservoir was built in 1975 to supply fresh water to the city of La Coruña. The surroundings of the reservoir are an attractive natural park
19th century country house, best known as the former summer residence of general and dictator Francisco Franco. A controversial place for its history and ownership
